agent1_raw: The leadership readout changes the objective, trains the evaluator on the reporting window, selects the threshold on the reporting cohort, and optimizes the challenger asymmetrically. All four of these are standard benchmark hygiene failures that, in combination, make the 4.5% estimate untrustworthy for a go/no-go decision.
agent2_raw: Three things happened simultaneously in this memo: the objective changed (renewal → margin), the evaluator was trained on the reporting window (not blind), and the threshold was selected on the same cohort that is reported. The fourth issue — asymmetric optimization — compounds this. The causal policy could still be superior; a clean evaluation would determine that. But the current numbers do not demonstrate it.
agent3_raw: The core benchmark problems are: objective misalignment (renewal vs margin), DR estimator not blind to the reporting window, threshold self-selection on the reporting cohort, and asymmetric calibration. The Finance-approved margin framing and the Bayesian hyperparameter search are not the problems per se — if both policies were evaluated under the same margin objective with the same optimization effort and a clean holdout, the comparison would be valid. The current comparison is not.
synthesis_raw: All three agents converge on empirical_test_agreed. The four benchmark defects are consistently identified. ETD: shared margin objective, nuisance models fit on pre-reporting data, threshold selected on pre-reporting validation, updated incumbent calibration, evaluation on frozen holdout with block-aware uncertainty.
all_issues_raised: Objective changed from renewal probability to net contribution margin
DR nuisance models trained on full experiment log including Q3-Q4 reporting period
Operating cutoff selected on Q3-Q4 cohort used for headline reporting
Asymmetric optimization: Bayesian tuning for challenger, frozen 18-month calibration for incumbent

empirical_test.condition: Refit nuisance models on pre-reporting data, preselect threshold, re-calibrate incumbent to margin objective, evaluate both on frozen holdout with block-aware uncertainty by region and week
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Challenger lift falls below zero or CI includes zero on clean holdout
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Challenger beats re-optimized incumbent by at least 3% incremental profit with block-aware 95% CI excluding zero
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Positive but sub-3% lift, or heterogeneous gains by region with some negative
